summary:
This case involves an obese teenage male with acute joint pain affecting multiple regions including hips/groin/knees, yet lacking systemic symptoms such as fever or erythema suggesting infection. Due to absence of warning signs indicating sepsis or significant trauma—and based upon age-specific patterns where mechanical causes dominate in youth—a conservative approach focusing on rest, symptom control, and monitoring appears most appropriate while avoiding unnecessary invasive procedures until further evaluation confirms underlying etiology.

Here goes:

A 13-year-old with acute multi-joint pain but no fever, redness, or other signs of infection makes septic arthritis unlikely despite common presentations in younger patients. In the context of obesity-induced biomechanical stress and lack of concerning features, supportive care and close observation remain safe first-line management before more aggressive steps.
